title: GBP Lander, GBP & Site Phone Numbers - Client Communication Needed
description:
Hey ,

Similar situation here with Sunset as we saw with Henco. 

We’re preparing to implement the footer updates, but we’ve identified discrepancies with the Portland phone numbers between the website and the GBP. 

What we’re seeing:
  • Site is using different hardcoded numbers for call swapping in the footer and on the Portland landing page, both of which do not match the GBP
  • Portland GBP secondary number is currently being used across all citations
  • Portland GBP is not linking to the location page

Under normal circumstances, we would align everything to the citation number and update the site's NAP accordingly for consistency.

Before finalizing, we need the client to confirm the correct official local number for Portland (i.e., which number should be used online for NAP consistency). 

To avoid delays, we will proceed using Portland’s secondary GBP number — since it is already consistent across citations — unless we’re advised otherwise. 

Can you please reach out ASAP to: 
  • Inform them we will proceed with the citation-consistent number in the new footer
  • Confirm the correct local number for Portland
  • Ensure Gasworks updates Portland GBP's landing page links accordingly

Once confirmed, we’ll finalize alignment across the site and GBP.
 
Thanks!

Here is all of the information:

Hardcoded in Portland's content - 503-773-6695
Hardcoded in the footer - 503-500-5866

GBP Primary - (503) 500-5855

Location Page  - https://www.sunsethc.com/portland/

By checking this box, I consent to receive marketing and promotional calls, and emails from or on behalf of  Sunset Heating & Cooling and its affiliates using an automated system or auto dialer for any purpose, including heating, cooling, electrical and plumbing. SMS Privacy

Mobile information will not be shared with third parties/affiliates for marketing or promotional purposes. Text messaging opt-in data and consent will not be shared with any third parties. 


4. Create a new page for each of the sites. This is the page that is linked to from the "Terms" link in the new SMS form disclaimer:

https://brand-name/sms-terms/

SMS/Messaging & Opt-Out
By opting in to messaging from Sunset Heating & Cooling, you agree to receive appointment reminders, informational, marketing and other types of messages from us. You can also reply HELP for assistance, or info@sunsethc.com. You may also opt out or request assistance by calling Sunset Heating & Cooling at (503) 500-5866. title: Sunset Website Edits
description:
Hi, seeking help if you can please take a look at the info provided by the client.

I have added the email verbatim to be sure:
---
I reviewed a few audit items that need attention on the Sunset website. Can you have your team review the items below? 

1. Indexable Pages
Several pages are duplicates or have no content and shouldn’t be indexed. Examples:
  • https://www.sunsethc.com/news-media/
  • https://www.sunsethc.com/monthly-special/
  • https://www.sunsethc.com/thank-you-for-being-our-neighbor/
Thank-you pages are also indexable and shouldn’t be.

2. Internal Linking
The homepage isn’t linked within the content of main service pages. Adding these links could help boost performance.

3. Heading Tags
Some H1s only say “HVAC services in [location].” These could be improved by including more specific keywords like a combination of heating, air conditioning, furnace, or heat pump.

4. Page Titles
Current titles split the keyword and service area with a | divider. There are also variations of the brand name. Some titles include “Call Sunset!” while others use “Sunset Heating & Cooling.”

Titles should follow this format:
Main service + in main geo | Sunset
If there’s room, use “Sunset Heating & Cooling.”

5. Orphan Pages
Pages under https://www.sunsethc.com/press-releases/ are all orphan pages.

6. Images
There are 34 images over 150KB, with some as large as 500KB.

7. Awards
No awards are listed on the homepage or on a dedicated awards page.

8. Duplicate Pages
Several pages scored 90% or more on the near duplicate test. These pages will need to be reviewed and modified to be unique. Here are the dupl. pages:
https://www.sunsethc.com/news-media/
https://www.sunsethc.com/thank-you-form-submitted/

9. Content Optimization
There are lots of opportunities for improvements. There are keyword cannibalization issues going on, poorly rewritten and duplicate content, bad internal linking strategies, etc. Key pages should be reviewed and it's content redone to follow best practices. This will need to be expanded into a larger project.

Thanks much!

title: Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites
description:
Hi, this is inline with previous task - Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites - J389 Go Arco

Here is a sheet we can also use for reference: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1qPfUUC1J2r1s7Ot8Y1PmIcje6WpyMszDhxHGz4TxDTk/edit?gid=0#gid=0

SEO added more info column D. 

I have included the task info here:
--- 
Hey Team!

To enhance our technical SEO baseline, improve crawl efficiency, and ensure consistent signaling across all client properties, It would be great to come to an agreement to outlining a new, standardized protocol for crawl management.

My suggestion is as follows:

Implement the following three directives on all client websites as part of our standard build and maintenance process.

1. XML Sitemap in robots.txt (Primary Directive)

This is the most effective way to inform search engines of all indexable URLs.

Action: Ensure the robots.txt file for every client domain contains a Sitemap directive pointing to the full URL of the XML sitemap.

Implementation Example:
User-agent: 
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap.xml

The "Why": This provides a clear, direct path for Google to find and crawl our most important pages, ensuring faster discovery and indexing of new content.

2. User-Facing HTML Sitemap (Footer)

This is for user experience and secondary crawl pathing.

Action: All client websites must have a user-facing HTML sitemap.

Implementation: This sitemap must be linked in the footer of every page on the site.

The "Why": This provides a clear navigation path for users, improves site accessibility, and   acts as a secondary crawl path for search engines to discover deep pages.

Strategic Crawl & Indexation Control

This is critical. We must be precise in telling crawlers what to crawl and what to index.

A. To Control Crawling (Using robots.txt)

We must Disallow crawlers from accessing low-value, non-SEO pages to conserve crawl budget.

Action: Audit and update the robots.txt file to Disallow non-public or utility sections of the site.
Implementation Examples:
Disallow: /wp-admin/

The WP sitemap page  plugin in perfect to use as a plugin for now

Regarding providing a robot.txt:

They differ per client especially regarding disallows but here is a comprehensive one that can be edited according to client needs especially regarding disallows:

User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap_index.xml

Also, just an example of placement. Sitemap listed at the very bottom of the footer on the left followed by privacy policy etc.

* In the footer we will use the page /sitemap/ and not the XML sitemap.  The XML sitemap is for Google & the robots.txt.

description:
Hey guys, I really like how cleanly laid out the Roth website is. And Wolfers has a lot of similar formatting. Can you please send these edits to Clixsy to make to the Sunset website? Feel free to challenge any of the items I have listed below. Thank you!


 


Toolbar Reformatting:

  • Let’s remove the ductless pump page from the toolbar on the website’s main page. Ductless mini splits can be an option beneath heating and air conditioning
  • I’d like to consolidate all services on the main page, like Roth’s website, so when you hover over top, you have the option to select from Heating, Air Conditioning, Plumbing, Drains, Electrical and Generators
  • Let’s remove the financing option under the About Us section and add it as a main page on the toolbar. I want to also include application integrations so customers can apply online. What do you need in order to do that?
  • Please rename Discounts to Promotions
  • Let’s add a page for our Membership Offering on the top
  • Let’s make Careers it’s own option at the top as well, removing it from the About Us section
  • Remove the Contact page from the top tool bar
  • Under the About Us section, I want to add our guarantees and our referral program section -

 


So, the main tool bar will now list About Us, Services, Membership, Financing, Promotions, Careers. With there now being more room at the top, let’s add the 100 year old logo to the right side of the page.


 


Main page reformatting:

  • Google reviews on the main page lists 1100 – can we update to current count?
  • I like how the contact us form is right at the top of the Roth page. Can we do the same by adding to the left-hand side of the main landing page and just shift the existing language over to the right?
  • Let’s replicate what’s listed under the Services section now, down below where we currently have “Furnace Installation, Furnace Repair and Maintenance, etc.” listed
  • Let’s replace the background photos with current promotions to rotate through and include a button for customers to book online now – rotate between a low monthly payment promo on a new furnace, a tune up promo, water heater promo and generator promo (see Right Now home page)
  • Incorporate the 100 year logo into the Book online button

title: Send Welcome Email
list_title: ONBOARDING › Pre-Discovery ADMIN / ACCOUNT MANAGER / PM
description:
Dear XXXXX (Client Contact),

On behalf of the entire Clixsy staff, I’d like to take this opportunity to welcome you as a new client. We are thrilled to have you with us.

I am also happy to say that I will be your primary point of contact, and please get in touch at any time with your questions, comments and feedback.

NEW CLIENT INTAKE FORM
 
I have linked you to our New Client Intake Form, below. Can you, or a designated individual, please fill it in and submit at your earliest convenience?
 
This New Client Intake Form forms the basis for our Discovery and initial kick-off meeting in which we’ll get together to begin working together and establishing roles, responsibilities and expectations. 
 
Please ensure you thoroughly fill in what you can, and if you are not able to fill something in, just let us know and we’ll work with you to either obtain the information or determine whether it’s a priority at this time. 
 
There are sections with requests for technical information like usernames and passwords for your online assets. Please try to be as accurate as possible with these so we can get in and audit your website/social/advertising accounts without having to return to you with further requests. This may slow the process up and cost us valuable time.
 
 Don’t worry if you can’t, or are unwilling to disclose all the information we have requested as the main goal here is to have at hand any resources we’ll need to work efficiently and to ensure that we organize and maintain current/accurate information for you so that you’ll never have to say “I don’t know” when a vendor or internal partner/employee asks “who has the login to XYZ”. Not to mention any information you can supply will help get the best returns we can from your investment with us. Just try and be as accurate and as thorough as you can. We will cover everything in more depth at your Discovery Meeting. 
 
http://www.clixsy.com/intake/

DISCOVERY MEETING
 
As soon as we have received and processed your New Client Intake Form, I will reach out to find a time to hold your Discovery Meeting. This will be held online via a Zoom call. 

 In the Discovery Meeting we will answer any questions you may have and talk about who on our team addresses the various roles and responsibilities, what next steps will be and establish our “battle rhythm” as our teams determine how best to work in unison. We will use the New Client Intake Form to discuss things like, your business and campaign goals, KPIs, customer journeys through your digital assets, your competitors etc. We will brainstorm the best methods to reach your goals and agree a ‘Definition of DONE’, for each stage of the process. 
 
We will also discuss any legal advertising constraints there may be in your industry and set boundaries based on what your company culture is comfortable with, and what is appropriate in the market. 
 
This should give us all the information we need to get started our work in increasing your market share and reaching your business goals. 
 
Once again, thank you for entrusting Clixsy with your business. Together we'll do our best to get you as big an ROI and slice of the market share as we can, as quickly as possible,
 
 
 Best regards,
 
 
 
 R. Corey Vandenberg 
President 
Corey@clixsy.com 
801-645-6466
